_comment: Lasing basic test: 1um n=1.5 guide -> 10um ISOTROPIC dye-doped n=1.5 reservoir -> 1um n=1.5 guide. Gaussian pulse at 500nm from the left of guide_1; input/output 1Ddft sensors mid-guide; atom-level (concentration) sensor across the reservoir.

reservoir._comment: Isotropic dye-doped medium: uniform n=1.5, no LC constants, no relaxation, no lc_fields.npz; gain via the sted block (pre-inverted 4-level atom).

sted._comment: Pre-inverted 4-level dye (N3=25 upper, N1=0 lower) — signal sees gain directly, no pump needed

source_1._comment: Focused Gaussian beam PULSE at 500nm (w0=3um waist, focused 6um downstream at the reservoir center) injected at the LEFT edge of guide_1 — replaces the old flat plane-wave launch, which excited the guide/reservoir's full multimode superposition (visible as an X-shaped interference/beating pattern in the concentration data) with a genuinely single-lobed beam.

conc_monitor._comment: Atom-level sensor: saves N(t) for all 4 dye levels over the reservoir; snap_interval in MEEP time units (1 unit = 3.336 fs)

snapshot_1._comment: Sparse field snapshots over the reservoir: every dt=50 MEEP units (~167 fs) -> ~7 snaps x ~8 MB, keeps the npz well under 100 MB
